name: Tbilisi Transport Company processed GTFS schedule
executiveSummary: Transitous republishes a processed GTFS Schedule snapshot for Tbilisi containing full-size bus, minibus, both metro lines and three gondola/ropeway services. It includes stops, route metadata, dated service calendars, trips, ordered stop times, and English/Georgian translations. This is schedule and network-reference data, not live movement. The processed feed deliberately omits shapes.txt because the source configuration sets drop-shapes=true.
provider: Transitous community mirror; upstream identified as Tbilisi Transport Company
geography: Tbilisi municipal public-transport network and outer municipal settlements
updateCadence: Rolling static schedule; HTTP Last-Modified was 2026-07-21 03:39:32 UTC and calendar coverage was 2026-07-21 through 2026-08-18
accessSummary: Unauthenticated GTFS ZIP over HTTPS. The tested processed file contained seven UTF-8 CSV tables. Transitous requests visible source attribution and recommends direct GTFS downloads instead of repeated journey-API calls.
license: No license information is declared for the underlying TTC feed; Transitous processing does not create an open-data license
recordCount: 1 agency; 2,374 stops; 284 routes; 9 calendars; 63,641 trips; 1,782,496 stop times; 5,318 translations

accessPoints.label: Listed upstream mirror
accessPoints.notes: Listed in Transitous configuration; processed output was the validated artifact

accessPoints.label: Transitous source configuration
accessPoints.notes: Declares drop-shapes=true and no feed license

schemas.description: One feed publisher/operator record.

schemas.description: Transit stops and stations across bus, minibus, metro and gondola modes.

schemas.description: Bus, minibus, metro and aerial-lift route records.

fields.notes: ID prefix distinguishes bus from minibus

schemas.description: Recurring weekday service patterns and the current short schedule horizon.

schemas.name: trips.txt
schemas.description: One directional scheduled trip on a route and service pattern.

schemas.name: stop_times.txt
schemas.description: Scheduled times at each ordered stop of each trip.

fields.notes: Starts at zero in this feed

schemas.name: translations.txt
schemas.description: English and Georgian translations for routes, stops and trip headsigns.

fields.notes: May be blank under GTFS field-value selection rules

samples.label: Three transport modes from routes.txt
samples.value:
route_long_name,route_short_name,agency_id,route_type,route_id,route_color
მ/ს ახმეტელი - მ/ს ვარკეთილი,1,TTC,1,1:Metro_Metro_1,FF505B
ავშნის III შეს. - მ/ს "ახმეტელის თეატრი",508,TTC,3,1:minibusR2518,0033B4

caveats: Processed community mirror, not an official TTC open-data publication
No shapes.txt or calendar_dates.txt in the validated processed snapshot
Underlying TTC data license is unclear
Static times do not prove that a vehicle is operating or on time
The 28-day calendar horizon requires regular refresh
reliability: HTTP metadata and every CSV table were inspected on 2026-07-22
Counts and route-type distributions were calculated from the downloaded ZIP
Use calendar.txt and HTTP headers rather than relying only on catalog metadata
privacy: Contains public network and schedule data, not passenger or fare-validation records

name: Tbilisi GTFS-Realtime bus and minibus vehicle positions
executiveSummary: A community-operated GTFS-Realtime v2.0 feed listed by Transitous publishes timestamped positions for ordinary Tbilisi buses and municipal minibuses. It supplies route and vehicle IDs, WGS84 coordinates, bearing, a per-vehicle observation timestamp, service date, and sometimes a stop and trip ID. During validation every entity was a VehiclePosition; no metro, gondola, TripUpdate or Alert entities were present.
provider: Community-operated vm-gtfs.spline.de source listed by Transitous; underlying data appears to originate from TTC passenger systems
geography: Tbilisi ordinary bus and minibus network
updateCadence: Continuously regenerated; two requests three seconds apart had different bytes and entity counts
accessSummary: Unauthenticated GTFS-Realtime v2.0 FeedMessage over HTTP. Decode with official GTFS-Realtime Protocol Buffer bindings. The source is HTTP-only; HTTPS failed during validation.
license: No license is declared in the Transitous configuration for this realtime source
recordCount: 1,275 VehiclePosition entities in the first sample and 1,274 three seconds later; 652 minibus-route IDs and 623 ordinary-bus route IDs

accessPoints.label: Raw GTFS-Realtime feed
accessPoints.notes: HTTP only; no published rate limit

schemas.name: Feed header
schemas.description: Feed-wide GTFS-Realtime metadata.

fields.meaning: Each response replaces the previous full state

schemas.name: VehiclePosition entity
schemas.description: One observed bus or minibus and its current location.

fields.notes: Same as vehicle ID in the sample

fields.meaning: Heading in degrees clockwise from north

fields.notes: 16 seconds behind the feed header in the sample

fields.meaning: Current or next associated stop according to the producer
fields.notes: Blank for 458 of 1,275 entities; exact semantics undocumented

schemas.name: Standard VehiclePosition fields not populated
schemas.description: Fields defined by GTFS-Realtime but absent in every tested entity.

samples.label: Decoded VehiclePosition
samples.note: JSON rendering of a Protocol Buffer entity decoded with official GTFS-Realtime bindings

caveats: Community-hosted and HTTP-only, creating integrity and uptime risks
No stated reuse license or published rate limit
stopId and tripId are often blank
No speed, occupancy, congestion, accessibility or position-accuracy fields
No metro or gondola positions, TripUpdates or Alerts were observed
reliability: Two live responses three seconds apart changed from 1,275 to 1,274 entities
Header and per-vehicle timestamps were current at retrieval
The feed is FULL_DATASET, so consumers must replace state instead of appending
privacy: Vehicle IDs identify public-service vehicles, not riders
No passenger identity or occupancy values are present

name: Transitous normalized Tbilisi realtime and routing API
subcategory: Normalized departures, routing and map segments
executiveSummary: Transitous runs the open-source MOTIS engine over Tbilisi's static GTFS and raw vehicle-position feed. Its versioned JSON API exposes nearby stop events with both scheduled and realtime-adjusted times, stop and route metadata, journey planning, trip details, geocoding and map trip segments. It is simpler to consume than raw protobuf and does not require TTC's embedded application credential, but it is a volunteer-operated best-effort service and inherits upstream licensing and quality limits.
geography: Tbilisi municipal public transport within a worldwide Transitous service
updateCadence: Realtime-derived for buses and minibuses; schedule-only for metro and gondola in the tested data
accessSummary: Unauthenticated HTTPS JSON API documented by MOTIS OpenAPI. Send a meaningful User-Agent with application name/version/contact or a browser Referer with site contact information. FOSS and non-profit use is encouraged; contact maintainers before high-volume or expensive routing use and show Transitous/source attribution.
license: Transitous has a FOSS/non-profit usage policy and attribution requirements; every underlying feed and OpenStreetMap component retains its own terms, and the TTC source license is unclear
recordCount: Stop-times test returned 5 events; a separate 10-minute Tbilisi-wide map query returned 9,191 segments: 9,035 bus/minibus, 126 subway, 30 aerial lift, including 2,135 realtime bus segments

accessPoints.label: Nearby stop times
accessPoints.authentication: None; identifying User-Agent or Referer required by policy
accessPoints.notes: Tested parameters: center, radius, n and language

accessPoints.label: Map trip segments
accessPoints.authentication: None; identifying User-Agent or Referer required by policy
accessPoints.notes: Accepts zoom, min/max bounds, startTime/endTime, precision and languages; this returns trip segments, not raw vehicle dots

schemas.name: Stop-times response
schemas.description: Top-level response for nearby stop events.

fields.meaning: Arrival and departure events around the requested point

schemas.name: Stop-time event
schemas.description: One route arrival/departure at a selected nearby stop.

fields.notes: Also SUBWAY and AERIAL_LIFT in current API

fields.meaning: Whether a realtime adjustment was applied

fields.example: Didi Dighomi IV M/D - Tbilisi State University H/B

fields.meaning: Boarding and alighting rule at the event

fields.notes: No cancellations observed and no Alert source existed upstream

fields.meaning: Whether the whole trip is canceled
Source GTFS file and line provenance

schemas.name: Event Place
schemas.description: Stop identity, coordinates, scheduled times and current returned times nested in an event.

fields.notes: Not passenger demand or social importance

fields.meaning: Current returned arrival, adjusted when realtime is available
Current returned departure, adjusted when realtime is available
Whether the event at this stop is canceled

schemas.name: Map trip segment
schemas.description: Scheduled or realtime-adjusted segment returned by /api/v6/map/trips; not a raw vehicle dot.

caveats: Volunteer best-effort service with no TTC SLA
Map-wide and routing queries can be expensive; coordinate high-volume use
Realtime flags are derived from the raw vehicle feed and inherit its gaps
A realTime=false event is schedule-only, not evidence that a service is absent
Underlying TTC feed has no declared open-data license
reliability: The nearby stop-times endpoint returned both scheduled and realtime-adjusted events
The OpenAPI schema and Transitous usage policy are public
Source-line provenance is included for GTFS-derived records
privacy: No passenger identities or fare validations are exposed
importance is a routing/search score and must not be interpreted as ridership

name: Official Georgian Railway schedule backend
subcategory: National rail schedules and station reference
executiveSummary: The current official Georgian Railway schedule page uses an unauthenticated JSON backend that exposes a multilingual station directory, all rides operating on a requested date, and a complete ordered list of railway points for each ride. Calling points contain scheduled times while infrastructure/pass-through points contain null times. This is detailed first-party schedule data, not live train tracking: no GPS position, actual or predicted time, delay, platform, cancellation reason or occupancy field was found.
geography: Georgia's railway network plus international and hidden station-directory records
updateCadence: Date-specific schedule queries and reference tables; /api/config/init also returns current server time
accessSummary: Unauthenticated JSON over HTTPS on the official gr.com.ge domain. The tested endpoints returned CORS Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *. The backend is undocumented, has no published rate limit and uses rideId—not the outer schedule record id or GUID—for ride details.
license: No explicit open-data license or API reuse terms were found; public technical access does not itself authorize republication
recordCount: 515 station-directory records; 34 rides for 2026-07-22; train 802 details contained 105 ordered railway points and 5 scheduled calling points

accessPoints.label: Date-specific ride summaries
accessPoints.notes: Page form also supplies startStationId and endStationId filters

accessPoints.label: Ride details
accessPoints.notes: Use list[].rideId, not list[].id or GUID

schemas.name: Station response wrapper
schemas.description: Pagination wrapper returned by /api/stations.

fields.meaning: Total station records including hidden and international points

fields.notes: Returned URLs used HTTP even though the request used HTTPS

schemas.name: Station
schemas.description: Multilingual directory record; may be hidden or outside Georgia.

fields.meaning: Country identifier serialized as a string
Whether the official UI hides the station

schemas.name: Ride-summary response
schemas.description: Date-specific schedules returned by /api/schedules/core.

fields.meaning: Rides on the requested service date

fields.notes: agregateValues is misspelled by the API

schemas.name: Ride summary
schemas.description: One train service operating on the requested date.

fields.meaning: Ride identifier required by the details endpoint

fields.notes: Observed codes: local, district and international
Contains id, code, name and cultureName

fields.meaning: Whether the schedule record is enabled
fields.notes: Not proof that the train is currently moving

fields.meaning: Number of scheduled calling or standing stations
fields.notes: Field name is misspelled in the API

schemas.name: Shared code/name object
schemas.description: Shape shared by route types, schedules and core station references.

fields.meaning: Entity code; meaning depends on parent object

schemas.name: Ride detail point
schemas.description: One ordered passenger or infrastructure railway point in /ride-details/{rideId}.

fields.notes: Unit undocumented; values behave like kilometres

fields.meaning: Country associated with the distance segment
Whether the train calls at this point

fields.notes: Zero at non-stops and the origin

caveats: Undocumented public-page backend with no version or rate-limit guarantee
No open-data/reuse license found
Scheduled times are not actual or predicted times
No GPS, delay, platform, cancellation reason, seat availability or occupancy fields
Station directory includes hidden and foreign records and mixed-quality transliterations
isActive is configuration state, not live movement
reliability: All endpoints returned current JSON from the official domain
The tested ride-details parameter was independently verified as rideId
Complete route-point ordering makes passenger calls distinguishable from pass-through infrastructure points
privacy: Contains schedules and public infrastructure records, not traveler bookings or passenger identities

name: Community-generated Georgian Railway GTFS
executiveSummary: Transitous publishes a community-generated Georgian Railway GTFS feed under ODbL-1.0. It contains 85 passenger stops, detailed railway shapes, 38 train-number routes, dated service exceptions, 416 generated trips, 4,089 calling times and stop-name translations in English, Georgian and Russian. Compared with the official Railway JSON backend it is openly licensed and map-ready; it is also derived rather than first-party and is not realtime.
provider: Jonah Brüchert / Transitous community, derived from Georgian Railway schedules and OpenStreetMap geometry
geography: Georgia's national rail network and scheduled international endpoints
updateCadence: Rolling schedule; HTTP Last-Modified was 2026-07-21 23:27:46 UTC and feed_info covered 2026-07-21 through 2026-11-17
accessSummary: Unauthenticated GTFS Schedule ZIP over HTTPS, generated by an open-source community pipeline. The ZIP has a duplicated attributions.txt member; robust consumers should tolerate duplicate ZIP member names while preserving the logical attribution row.
license: Open Database License 1.0 (ODbL-1.0); attributions.txt credits OpenStreetMap contributors
recordCount: 1 agency; 1 feed_info; 85 stops; 115,902 shape points; 38 routes; 4,179 calendar-date rows; 416 trips; 4,089 stop times; 1 logical attribution; 255 translations

schemas.name: agency.txt and feed_info.txt
schemas.description: Agency identity and generated-feed coverage metadata.

schemas.name: stops.txt
schemas.description: Passenger railway stations with coordinates.

fields.notes: Many IDs appear to be OpenStreetMap object IDs

schemas.name: shapes.txt
schemas.description: Detailed polyline points for generated national and international rail routes.

schemas.name: routes.txt
schemas.description: Train-number routes with standard and extended rail-mode types.

fields.notes: Observed: 2 standard rail, 102 long-distance rail, 106 regional rail
Matches the train number in the sample

schemas.name: calendar_dates.txt
schemas.description: Explicit dates on which each generated service runs.

fields.meaning: Service is added on this date

schemas.name: trips.txt
schemas.description: Generated dated rides mapped to train-number routes and shapes.

schemas.name: stop_times.txt
schemas.description: Scheduled passenger calling times ordered along each generated trip.

schemas.name: attributions.txt
schemas.description: Required source attribution; the ZIP contains this filename twice but one logical row.

schemas.name: translations.txt
schemas.description: Localized stop names; 85 stops in each of English, Georgian and Russian.

caveats: Community-derived rather than first-party publication
Not realtime; no delay, actual arrival, cancellation, platform, seat or live-position fields
route_long_name is blank for every route
Duplicate attributions.txt ZIP member can confuse simplistic ZIP readers
Critical schedules should be compared with the official Railway API/page
reliability: Every table was inspected and row-counted from the live processed ZIP
Four-month feed horizon is declared in feed_info
Shapes and calling times provide map-ready trip structure
Explicit ODbL and OpenStreetMap attribution are present
privacy: Contains public railway infrastructure and schedules, not bookings or traveler identity
